questionHtml:
Consider the Born–Haber cycle for the formation of sodium oxide:

What is the lattice enthalpy, in kJ mol−1, of sodium oxide?


A.  414 + 2(108) + 249 + 2(496) − 141 + 790

B.  414 + 2(108) + 249 + 2(496) + 141 + 790

C.  −414 + 2(108) + 249 + 2(496) − 141 + 790

D.  −414 − 2(108) − 249 − 2(496) + 141 − 790

meta.title: Consider the Born–Haber cycle for the formation of sodium oxide: What is the lattice enthalpy, in kJ mol−1, of sodium oxide? A. 414 + 2(108) + 249 + 2(496) − 141 + 790 B. 414 + 2(1
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.2.5—A Born–Haber cycle is an application of Hess’s law, used to show energy changes in the formation of an ionic compound. Interpret and determine values from a Born–Haber cycle for compounds composed of univalent and divalent ions.
